summary:
Vertical growth of the maxilla occurs through processes involving bone resorption and deposition along specific developmental pathways, primarily facilitated by fusion sites or sutures between bones. These biological mechanisms are essential for achieving appropriate facial proportions as an individual matures from infancy into adulthood. \boxed{\text{Sutural activity}}

The summary captures the essence of Agent2’s reasoning regarding what drives vertical maxillary growth while adhering strictly to focusing only on critical clinical details pertinent to answering why this particular aspect grows vertically over time. It highlights both mechanistic elements related to skeletal biology alongside their relevance within human ontogeny stages where these transformations become clinically observable phenomena affecting overall face shape characteristics.
